deep_snippets.text: ## Chapter 10 Cell Reproduction 10.1 Cell Division 10.2 The Cell Cycle 10.3 Control of the Cell Cycle 10.4 Cancer and the Cell Cycle 10.5 Prokaryotic Cell Division A human, like every sexually reproducing organism, begins life as a fertilized egg (embryo) or zygote. In our species, billions of cell divisions subsequently must occur in a controlled manner in order to produce a complex, multicellular human comprising trillions of cells. Thus, the original single-celled zygote is literally the ancestor of all cells in the body. However, once a human is fully grown, cell reproduction is still necessary to repair and regenerate tissues, and sometimes to increase our size! In fact, all multicellular organisms use cell division for growth and the maintenance and repair of cells and tissues. Cell division is closely regulated, and the occasional failure of this regulation can have life-threatening consequences. Single-celled organisms may also use cell division as their method of reproduction. 10.1 Cell Division

Telomerase and Aging Cells that undergo cell division continue to have their telomeres shortened because most somatic cells do not make telomerase. This essentially means that telomere shortening is associated with aging. With the advent of modern medicine, preventative health care, and healthier lifestyles, the human life span has increased, and there is an increasing demand for people to look younger and have a better quality of life as they grow older. In 2010, scientists found that telomerase can reverse some age-related conditions in mice. This may have potential in regenerative medicine.2 Telomerase-deficient mice were used in these studies; these mice have tissue atrophy, stem cell depletion, organ system failure, and impaired tissue injury responses. Telomerase reactivation in these mice caused extension of telomeres, reduced DNA damage, reversed neurodegeneration, and improved the function of the testes, spleen, and intestines. Thus, telomere reactivation may have potential for treating age-related diseases in humans. Cancer is characterized by uncontrolled cell division of abnormal cells. The cells accumulate mutations, proliferate uncontrollably, and can migrate to different parts of the body through a process called metastasis. Scientists have observed that cancerous cells have considerably shortened telomeres and that telomerase is active in these cells. Interestingly, only after the telomeres were shortened in the cancer cells did the telomerase become active. If the action of telomerase in these cells can be inhibited by drugs during cancer therapy, then the

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 细胞分裂综合

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物学
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 10
- **章节/领域**: 细胞的生命历程
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性
> 观察处于细胞周期不同阶段的细胞，结合有丝分裂模型，描述细胞增殖的主要特征
> 制作和观察根尖细胞有丝分裂简易装片，或观察其永久装片

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「细胞分裂综合」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「细胞分裂综合」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「细胞分裂综合」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞分裂综合」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞分裂综合」教学。
2. 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 围绕课标要点“2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞分裂综合」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系健康、生态、农业或生物技术案例，讨论「细胞分裂综合」与现实生活的关系。
- 通过模型图、显微图或调查资料拓展学生对生命系统的理解。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
细胞分裂综合是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞分裂综合」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: ## Chapter 10 Cell Reproduction 10.1 Cell Division 10.2 The Cell Cycle 10.3 Control of the Cell Cycle 10.4 Cancer and the Cell Cycle 10.5 Prokaryotic Cell Division A human, like every sexually reproducing organism, begins life as a fertilized egg (embryo) or zygote. In our species, billions of cell divisions subsequently must occur in a controlled manner in order to produce a complex, multicellular human comprising trillions of cells. Thus, the original single-celled zygote is literally the ancestor of all cells in the body. However, once a human is fully grown, cell reproduction is still necessary to repair and regenerate tissues, and sometimes to increase our size! In fact, all multicellular organisms use cell division for growth and the maintenance and repair of cells and tissues. Cell division is closely regulated, and the occasional failure of this regulation can have life-threatening consequences. Single-celled organisms may also use cell division as their method of reproduction. 10.1 Cell Division

Telomerase and Aging Cells that undergo cell division continue to have their telomeres shortened because most somatic cells do not make telomerase. This essentially means that telomere shortening is associated with aging. With the advent of modern medicine, preventative health care, and healthier lifestyles, the human life span has increased, and there is an increasing demand for people to look younger and have a better quality of life as they grow older. In 2010, scientists found that telomerase can reverse some age-related conditions in mice. This may have potential in regenerative medicine.2 Telomerase-deficient mice were used in these studies; these mice have tissue atrophy, stem cell depletion, organ system failure, and impaired tissue injury responses. Telomerase reactivation in these mice caused extension of telomeres, reduced DNA damage, reversed neurodegeneration, and improved the function of the testes, spleen, and intestines. Thus, telomere reactivation may have potential for treating age-related diseases in humans. Cancer is characterized by uncontrolled cell division of abnormal cells. The cells accumulate mutations, proliferate uncontrollably, and can migrate to different parts of the body through a process called metastasis. Scientists have observed that cancerous cells have considerably shortened telomeres and that telomerase is active in these cells. Interestingly, only after the telomeres were shortened in the cancer cells did the telomerase become active. exercises.stem: 依据课标「细胞分裂综合」：2.3.1 描述细胞通过不同的方式进行分裂，其中有丝分裂保证了遗传信息在亲代和子代细胞中的一致性 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：观察处于细胞周期不同阶段的细胞，结合有丝分裂模型，描述细胞增殖的主要特征
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「细胞分裂综合」相关的生命现象。
